fact: Port Royal was built on a sand spit, with heavy European brick construction on loose, wet sand
source: Port Royal, Jamaica (Wikipedia), citing archaeological and geological studies of the site

fact: Port Royal had approximately two thousand buildings packed onto the narrow strip of land
source: Port Royal Rediscovered, Robert F. Marx (1973) — one of the foundational archaeological accounts of Port Royal

fact: The 1692 earthquake caused soil liquefaction, causing buildings and streets to sink into the ground
source: "The Port Royal Earthquake" — Earthquake Engineering Research Institute; also documented in the 1692 earthquake Wikipedia article

fact: Two-thirds of the city was destroyed or submerged in the earthquake
source: 1692 Jamaica earthquake (Wikipedia), citing contemporary accounts including Rev. Emmanuel Heath's letter and other primary sources

fact: The destruction occurred in approximately three minutes
source: Contemporary eyewitness accounts, notably Rev. Emmanuel Heath's letter; widely cited in Marx, Port Royal Rediscovered (1973) and in the National Library of Jamaica archives

fact: Port Royal was known as the richest port in the Caribbean (sometimes called 'the wickedest city on Earth')
source: Widely documented characterization; see Pawson, David & Buisseret, David, Port Royal, Jamaica (2000, University of the West Indies Press)
